It looks cool but that music choice for the trailer was awful. For a couple reasons. First of all it doesn't fit the setting at all. It felt so out of place. Second, and this is my opinion, Ed Sheeran seems like a cool guy but I think his music is god awful. And finally that song was ridiculously popular a couple months ago. You don't put recently popular music into movie trailers. It's just a bad idea. 

Now the first HTTYD is one of my favorite movies of all time. Beautiful score, character dynamic between Hiccup and Toothless, and great visuals. I hope this one ditches the poppy music and goes back to its roots with the indie style they had in the first movie. That feels more authentic.

This franchise is one of the best thing to ever exist. I watched this with my mumwho loves the franchise just as much as I do, holding her hand, and we both cried. I sobbed like a literal baby. I couldn't even see some parts due to my tears. This franchise has affected my life massively. I wouldn't be where I am today or be the person I am if it wasn't for that first film. I've never had such a strong attachment to any media ever. Words can't explain what I'm feeling, and how much these films mean to me, they're my world. I feel happy I feel sad, I feel excited, all mushed together. I just can't describe it. I've never felt this way about anything. I'm just absolutely ecstatic, yet a complete and utter wreck at the same time. I want more than anything for this film to have a happy ending, I won't be able to bare Toothless and Hiccup parting. I just don't even know what to say. This is going to be an absolutely amazing film, dare I say, possibly the best in the franchise. ALL ABOARD THE EMOTIONAL ROLLER-COASTER. THE CAPS ARE HERE, I JUST CAN'T COMPREHEND MY FEELINGS. HERE'S TO THE END OF A PHENOMENAL FRANCHISE
